rationale: Both candidates correctly zero in on the only substantive ground-truth point (the already_submitted gate removal is safe because both submit paths reject list prompts identically) and both flag the missing regression test as a non-blocking gap, matching the LGTM/no-blocker sentiment of the real reviewers. X presents this more cleanly with a standard blocker-scan table, an explicit APPROVE verdict matching the actual merged/approved outcome, and a sharper articulation of why the old gating specifically broke submit_initial. Y's findings are equally grounded and its suggested regression test is slightly more concrete (exact test location/assertions), but the report is cluttered with low-value 'sweep'/re-read bullets and lands on a COMMENT verdict that reads more cautious than the ground truth warrants, giving it a marginally lower actionability/precision edge.
